scenarios.label: Florida shallow natural lake, winter control month 1

expectation.seasonal_story: Florida winter largemouth should stay in a controlled lower-column lane with only mild daily openings.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Drop it into cover or along grass edges and count it down; short one-inch hops with a pause mimics a crawfish on the bottom — don't rush the next cast. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Drop it into cover or along grass edges and count it down; short one-inch hops with a pause mimics a crawfish on the bottom — don't rush the next cast. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. It stays low in the zone where this day still wants fish to hold. It gives you a different bottom contact look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Work it with slow rod sweeps across rocky bottom, letting it tick and grind the structure rather than hop above it. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Jerk, jerk, pause. The bait should dart and glide sideways on each twitch; dial the pause length to water temperature — colder means longer. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: Cold metabolism shuts down activity. Slow and low is the only play. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Cast across and swing it on a tight line through the drift, then activate on the hang-down with short strips before lifting. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Cold metabolism shuts down activity. Slow and low is the only play. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Cast across and swing it on a tight line through the drift, then activate on the hang-down with short strips before lifting. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Cold metabolism shuts down activity. Slow and low is the only play. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It gives you a different fly bottom look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Cast upstream and mend for a dead-drift first; then come alive with quick, short strips that make the claw materials flex like a fleeing crawfish. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip in short, consistent pulls so the dumbbell eyes make the fly jig up and down; let it sink between strips to stay hook-point up throughout. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

scenarios.label: Florida shallow natural lake, prespawn opening month 2

expectation.seasonal_story: Florida prespawn largemouth should open moving baitfish lanes without abandoning controlled shallow cover options.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Work it parallel to grass lines or over submerged timber; slow the retrieve near the target and let the head tick the top. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Work it parallel to grass lines or over submerged timber; slow the retrieve near the target and let the head tick the top. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It gives you a different horizontal search look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Cast past targets and swim it through, reeling just fast enough to feel the head wiggle; slow down near the edge of cover and let it drop. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Jerk, jerk, pause. The bait should dart and glide sideways on each twitch; dial the pause length to water temperature — colder means longer. Keep it high in the zone.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Strip with alternating long and short pulls so the articulated body undulates differently each time; kill the retrieve if you see a fish following and let it sink. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip with alternating long and short pulls so the articulated body undulates differently each time; kill the retrieve if you see a fish following and let it sink. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It gives you a different fly baitfish look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip in short, consistent pulls so the dumbbell eyes make the fly jig up and down; let it sink between strips to stay hook-point up throughout.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Use varied strip lengths with hard pauses after each burst; the fly should surge, then stall and hover like a wounded meal.

scenarios.label: Florida shallow natural lake, spawn postspawn transition month 4

expectation.seasonal_story: Florida spawn and immediate postspawn should stay shallow and target-oriented first, with one clean baitfish follow-up lane; hollow-body frog can join only on legitimately open aggressive spring days around shallow cover.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Cast to cover and give it slack on the fall; if nothing bites, drag it along the bottom in slow sweeps and pause any time you feel resistance. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Cast to cover and give it slack on the fall; if nothing bites, drag it along the bottom in slow sweeps and pause any time you feel resistance. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It gives you a different cover weedless look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Get the bait right into the cover before moving it — thumb the spool on the drop, and if you don't feel a strike, give it one or two hops then move on. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It stays in the middle band where the seasonal setup is most stable today. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Cast past targets and swim it through, reeling just fast enough to feel the head wiggle; slow down near the edge of cover and let it drop. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip in short, consistent pulls so the dumbbell eyes make the fly jig up and down; let it sink between strips to stay hook-point up throughout. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Bright light trims the day back toward cleaner looks. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Cast upstream and mend for a dead-drift first; then come alive with quick, short strips that make the claw materials flex like a fleeing crawfish. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

scenarios.label: Florida shallow natural lake, summer positioning month 6

expectation.seasonal_story: Early- to midsummer Florida largemouth should lean grass, cover, and aggressive surface-adjacent lanes, but prop bait is still a supporting option until the cleaner late-summer cadence truly opens.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Cast past targets and swim it through, reeling just fast enough to feel the head wiggle; slow down near the edge of cover and let it drop. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Cast past targets and swim it through, reeling just fast enough to feel the head wiggle; slow down near the edge of cover and let it drop. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It gives you a different horizontal search look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Swim it at the speed where you feel a steady kick through the rod; use rod angle to change depth without changing retrieve speed. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: The monthly surface window is still alive today, even with a little ripple on top. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Twitch it just enough to make the legs kick; pause over every opening in the cover and count to three before moving — strikes on mats come after the pause. Keep it on top.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Strip with alternating long and short pulls so the articulated body undulates differently each time; kill the retrieve if you see a fish following and let it sink. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip with alternating long and short pulls so the articulated body undulates differently each time; kill the retrieve if you see a fish following and let it sink. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: The monthly surface window is still alive today, even with a little ripple on top. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It gives you a different fly surface look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Walk it across the surface with alternating rod twitches; slow to a crawl near pad edges and let it rest fully before the next move. Keep it on top.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: The monthly surface window is still alive today, even with a little ripple on top. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Swim it on a constant slow retrieve just fast enough to leave a wake; target near-shore edges and structure where big fish expect food to cross. Keep it on top.

scenarios.label: Florida shallow natural lake, winter control month 12

expectation.seasonal_story: Florida winter largemouth should stay in a controlled lower-column lane with only mild daily openings.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. It stays low in the zone where this day still wants fish to hold.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Work it with slow rod sweeps across rocky bottom, letting it tick and grind the structure rather than hop above it. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. It stays low in the zone where this day still wants fish to hold.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Work it with slow rod sweeps across rocky bottom, letting it tick and grind the structure rather than hop above it. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Drop it into cover or along grass edges and count it down; short one-inch hops with a pause mimics a crawfish on the bottom — don't rush the next cast. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Jerk, jerk, pause. The bait should dart and glide sideways on each twitch; dial the pause length to water temperature — colder means longer. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Cast across and swing it on a tight line through the drift, then activate on the hang-down with short strips before lifting. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Cast across and swing it on a tight line through the drift, then activate on the hang-down with short strips before lifting. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It gives you a different fly baitfish look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip in short, consistent pulls so the dumbbell eyes make the fly jig up and down; let it sink between strips to stay hook-point up throughout. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Cast upstream and mend for a dead-drift first; then come alive with quick, short strips that make the claw materials flex like a fleeing crawfish. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

scenarios.label: Texas reservoir, winter control month 1

expectation.seasonal_story: South-central winter reservoir largemouth should stay lower and controlled, with jerkbait or flat-side still in play when conditions allow.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: Heat stress moves fish to cooler, deeper water and narrows the feeding window. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Drag it along hard bottom and ledges so the flat head kicks and rocks; lift only slightly on the pull so the trailer stays near the substrate. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Heat stress moves fish to cooler, deeper water and narrows the feeding window. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Drag it along hard bottom and ledges so the flat head kicks and rocks; lift only slightly on the pull so the trailer stays near the substrate. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Heat stress moves fish to cooler, deeper water and narrows the feeding window. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It gives you a different horizontal search look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Swim it at the speed where you feel a steady kick through the rod; use rod angle to change depth without changing retrieve speed. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Heat stress moves fish to cooler, deeper water and narrows the feeding window. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Work it with wrist snaps, not big sweeps; keep slack in the line between snaps so the bait swings freely, then pause until you see a follow or feel weight. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: Heat stress moves fish to cooler, deeper water and narrows the feeding window. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Fish it under an indicator or on a slow hand-twist so the fly hovers level and pulses in place rather than diving nose-first. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Heat stress moves fish to cooler, deeper water and narrows the feeding window. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Fish it under an indicator or on a slow hand-twist so the fly hovers level and pulses in place rather than diving nose-first. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Heat stress moves fish to cooler, deeper water and narrows the feeding window. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It gives you a different fly bottom look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Cast across and swing it on a tight line through the drift, then activate on the hang-down with short strips before lifting. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Heat stress moves fish to cooler, deeper water and narrows the feeding window. It stays low in the zone where this day still wants fish to hold. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Swim it slowly through deeper pockets and woody structure; the rabbit strip breathes on a semi-tight line, so use the absolute minimum retrieve speed. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

scenarios.label: Texas reservoir, prespawn opening month 2

expectation.seasonal_story: South-central prespawn reservoirs should open baitfish lanes when conditions allow, but cold-front days can still pull the lead back to football jig and other bottom-control reads.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Drag it along hard bottom and ledges so the flat head kicks and rocks; lift only slightly on the pull so the trailer stays near the substrate. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Drag it along hard bottom and ledges so the flat head kicks and rocks; lift only slightly on the pull so the trailer stays near the substrate. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Cold metabolism shuts down activity. Slow and low is the only play. It stays low in the zone where this day still wants fish to hold. It gives you a different cover weedless look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Flip or pitch into cover, let the craw fall straight on semi-slack line, then hop it once or twice before the next pitch. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Yo-yo the blade: snap the rod so the head kicks and vibrates, then let it pendulum back with a controlled semi-slack fall. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: A cooling trend tightens fish and shifts preference lower and slower. It stays low in the zone where this day still wants fish to hold. It gives you a different fly bottom look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Let it sink to the strike zone then retrieve with long, slow strips; the rabbit hair undulates seductively between pulls, so don't rush the retrieve. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: A cooling trend tightens fish and shifts preference lower and slower. It stays low in the zone where this day still wants fish to hold. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Bump bottom with short strips and let the fly tick along rocks; keep tension so you feel every stop against structure. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

scenarios.label: Alabama river current system, winter control month 12

expectation.seasonal_story: Winter river largemouth should stay current-aware and controlled, not drift into lake-only or ultra-finesse nonsense.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: A warming trend nudges fish slightly higher in the allowed range. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Pendulum flip or pitch into heavy cover; let it free-fall on a semi-slack line, then hold and lift if the line jumps or goes heavy. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: A warming trend nudges fish slightly higher in the allowed range. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Pendulum flip or pitch into heavy cover; let it free-fall on a semi-slack line, then hold and lift if the line jumps or goes heavy. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

scenarios.label: Illinois backwater river, summer positioning month 6

expectation.seasonal_story: Summer Illinois backwater largemouth should absolutely allow slop and ambush tools like frog, swim jig, and spinnerbait before trying to get cute with clean-water finesse.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Cast past targets and swim it through, reeling just fast enough to feel the head wiggle; slow down near the edge of cover and let it drop.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Cast past targets and swim it through, reeling just fast enough to feel the head wiggle; slow down near the edge of cover and let it drop.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It gives you a different horizontal search look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Work it parallel to grass lines or over submerged timber; slow the retrieve near the target and let the head tick the top.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Snap the rod sideways so the bait darts left or right, then drop the rod tip so it glides and sinks on a slack line before the next snap.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Strip with alternating long and short pulls so the articulated body undulates differently each time; kill the retrieve if you see a fish following and let it sink.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip with alternating long and short pulls so the articulated body undulates differently each time; kill the retrieve if you see a fish following and let it sink.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It gives you a different fly baitfish look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip in short, consistent pulls so the dumbbell eyes make the fly jig up and down; let it sink between strips to stay hook-point up throughout.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Use varied strip lengths with hard pauses after each burst; the fly should surge, then stall and hover like a wounded meal.

scenarios.label: Illinois backwater river, fall transition month 11

expectation.seasonal_story: Late-fall Illinois backwater largemouth should tighten into slower wood, eddy, and current-break lanes, with compact jig control back in front of broad fall roaming.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: Heat stress moves fish to cooler, deeper water and narrows the feeding window. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Pendulum flip or pitch into heavy cover; let it free-fall on a semi-slack line, then hold and lift if the line jumps or goes heavy. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Heat stress moves fish to cooler, deeper water and narrows the feeding window. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Pendulum flip or pitch into heavy cover; let it free-fall on a semi-slack line, then hold and lift if the line jumps or goes heavy. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Heat stress moves fish to cooler, deeper water and narrows the feeding window. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It gives you a different reaction mid column look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Jerk, jerk, pause. The bait should dart and glide sideways on each twitch; dial the pause length to water temperature — colder means longer. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Heat stress moves fish to cooler, deeper water and narrows the feeding window. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Cast past the target and retrieve through it, varying speed to find the blade thump fish respond to; hesitate briefly at cover edges. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: It stays practical in current seams and river lanes when flow still matters. Woolly Bugger tracks well when leech_worm is a realistic meal.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: It stays practical in current seams and river lanes when flow still matters. Woolly Bugger tracks well when leech_worm is a realistic meal.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Heat stress moves fish to cooler, deeper water and narrows the feeding window. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It gives you a different fly baitfish look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Retrieve with medium-to-long pulls and let the feathers collapse and flare; pause every 3-4 strips to let the profile swell and hang for trailing fish. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Heat stress moves fish to cooler, deeper water and narrows the feeding window. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Retrieve with medium strips, then occasionally rip hard so the body compresses and then springs apart — pause after each hard strip and hold your nerve. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Work it with slow rod sweeps across rocky bottom, letting it tick and grind the structure rather than hop above it. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Work it with slow rod sweeps across rocky bottom, letting it tick and grind the structure rather than hop above it. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Cold metabolism shuts down activity. Slow and low is the only play. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It gives you a different cover weedless look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Drop it into cover or along grass edges and count it down; short one-inch hops with a pause mimics a crawfish on the bottom — don't rush the next cast. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Vertical jig it with short, sharp hops over suspended fish; the flutter-fall is the presentation — set hard the moment you feel weight. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Cast across and swing it on a tight line through the drift, then activate on the hang-down with short strips before lifting. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Cold metabolism shuts down activity. Slow and low is the only play. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Cast across and swing it on a tight line through the drift, then activate on the hang-down with short strips before lifting. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. It stays low in the zone where this day still wants fish to hold. It gives you a different fly bottom look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip with consistent medium pulls; the cone keeps the fly diving nose-first on each retrieve, so use pauses to let it hunt and settle before the next strip. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip in short, consistent pulls so the dumbbell eyes make the fly jig up and down; let it sink between strips to stay hook-point up throughout. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Bump bottom with short strips and let the fly tick along rocks; keep tension so you feel every stop against structure. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: A cooling trend tightens fish and shifts preference lower and slower. It stays low in the zone where this day still wants fish to hold. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.

scenarios.label: Georgia highland reservoir, summer positioning month 6

expectation.seasonal_story: Clear highland summer largemouth should lean toward deeper structure and cover fishing with finesse backup options.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Work it with a jerk-sink-jerk cadence; keep the line slightly slack between twitches so the bait moves unpredictably, and vary the pause length.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Work it with a jerk-sink-jerk cadence; keep the line slightly slack between twitches so the bait moves unpredictably, and vary the pause length.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. It stays in the middle band where the seasonal setup is most stable today. It gives you a different horizontal search look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Swim it at the speed where you feel a steady kick through the rod; use rod angle to change depth without changing retrieve speed.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Crawl it across rock, gravel, or shell beds with steady low lifts and long pauses — the football head keeps it upright and loud the whole time.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Cast and let the weighted eyes sink the fly, then retrieve with a strip-pause cadence that makes it dart upward on the strip and dive back on the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Cast and let the weighted eyes sink the fly, then retrieve with a strip-pause cadence that makes it dart upward on the strip and dive back on the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It gives you a different fly baitfish look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip with alternating long and short pulls so the articulated body undulates differently each time; kill the retrieve if you see a fish following and let it sink.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Lower light supports a slightly higher, more open lane. Crawfish Streamer tracks well when crawfish is a realistic meal. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Pinch the fly to the bottom and move it in erratic 1-2 inch hops with long pauses; the slower and lower, the better — crawfish don't sprint. Keep it low in the strike zone.

scenarios.label: Louisiana grass lake, prespawn opening month 3

expectation.seasonal_story: Dirty-to-stained southern prespawn grass bass should open visible grass and baitfish lanes without turning into winter bottom dragging; lipless can legitimately steal the lead on colder windy grass days.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It stays in the middle band where the seasonal setup is most stable today.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Pendulum flip or pitch into heavy cover; let it free-fall on a semi-slack line, then hold and lift if the line jumps or goes heavy. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It stays in the middle band where the seasonal setup is most stable today.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It gives you a different horizontal search look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Yo-yo it through open water: rip up sharply, let it flutter and sink on a semi-slack line, then rip again; vary fall depth by watching the line angle. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Slow-roll it along the bottom edge of cover at a steady pace, keeping the blades just ticking; bump any piece of structure and let it rise. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It stays in the middle band where the seasonal setup is most stable today.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It stays in the middle band where the seasonal setup is most stable today.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It gives you a different fly baitfish look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Use a mix of steady strips and sharp 6-inch twitches so the jointed body swims and stalls; pause when you see a follow. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Cast across or slightly downstream, let it jig toward you with short strips so the weighted eyes keep the fly tracking hook-point up. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

scenarios.label: Louisiana grass lake, summer positioning month 6

expectation.seasonal_story: Warm southern grass largemouth should still favor visible shallow-cover and baitfish lanes, but the current seasonal pool on this archived neutral June window is more soft-jerkbait, spinnerbait, swim-jig, and medium-crank than true frog-only surface behavior.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Cast past targets and swim it through, reeling just fast enough to feel the head wiggle; slow down near the edge of cover and let it drop. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Cast past targets and swim it through, reeling just fast enough to feel the head wiggle; slow down near the edge of cover and let it drop. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It gives you a different horizontal search look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Work it parallel to grass lines or over submerged timber; slow the retrieve near the target and let the head tick the top. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Cast past structure and crank it down, then steer it right into cover; the deflection is the trigger, so don't try to avoid contact. Keep it high in the zone.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Strip with alternating long and short pulls so the articulated body undulates differently each time; kill the retrieve if you see a fish following and let it sink. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip with alternating long and short pulls so the articulated body undulates differently each time; kill the retrieve if you see a fish following and let it sink. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It gives you a different fly baitfish look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Retrieve with medium-to-long pulls and let the feathers collapse and flare; pause every 3-4 strips to let the profile swell and hang for trailing fish. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Reduced visibility supports a stronger profile fish can find more easily. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip in short, consistent pulls so the dumbbell eyes make the fly jig up and down; let it sink between strips to stay hook-point up throughout.

expectation.seasonal_story: Clear Ozarks prespawn largemouth should still be disciplined, with jerkbait and flat-side leading the lane.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Drag it along hard bottom and ledges so the flat head kicks and rocks; lift only slightly on the pull so the trailer stays near the substrate. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Drag it along hard bottom and ledges so the flat head kicks and rocks; lift only slightly on the pull so the trailer stays near the substrate. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It stays low in the zone where this day still wants fish to hold. It gives you a different bottom contact look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Cast and let it fall to bottom, then drag very slowly with the rod held low; shake the tip just enough to quiver the tail without lifting the head. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Cold metabolism shuts down activity. Slow and low is the only play. It stays low in the zone where this day still wants fish to hold. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Flip or pitch into cover, let the craw fall straight on semi-slack line, then hop it once or twice before the next pitch. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.

top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip with alternating long and short pulls so the articulated body undulates differently each time; kill the retrieve if you see a fish following and let it sink. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Cast and let the weighted eyes sink the fly, then retrieve with a strip-pause cadence that makes it dart upward on the strip and dive back on the pause. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

scenarios.label: California Delta freshwater reach, summer positioning month 6

expectation.seasonal_story: Summer Delta largemouth should favor grass/current ambush lanes and visible cover tools, and on surface-closed archived river days a soft jerkbait can legitimately outrank the frog while spinnerbait and swim jig stay in the core story.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: It stays practical in current seams and river lanes when flow still matters. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Slow-roll it along the bottom edge of cover at a steady pace, keeping the blades just ticking; bump any piece of structure and let it rise. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: It stays practical in current seams and river lanes when flow still matters. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Slow-roll it along the bottom edge of cover at a steady pace, keeping the blades just ticking; bump any piece of structure and let it rise. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: The monthly surface window is still alive today, even with a little ripple on top. Hollow-Body Frog stays in play when bluegill_perch is relevant. It gives you a different cover weedless look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Twitch it just enough to make the legs kick; pause over every opening in the cover and count to three before moving — strikes on mats come after the pause. Keep it on top.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: It stays practical in current seams and river lanes when flow still matters. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Work it with a jerk-sink-jerk cadence; keep the line slightly slack between twitches so the bait moves unpredictably, and vary the pause length. Keep it high in the zone.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: Bright light trims the day back toward cleaner looks. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Use a mix of steady strips and sharp 6-inch twitches so the jointed body swims and stalls; pause when you see a follow. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Bright light trims the day back toward cleaner looks. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Use a mix of steady strips and sharp 6-inch twitches so the jointed body swims and stalls; pause when you see a follow. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: The monthly surface window is still alive today, even with a little ripple on top. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It gives you a different fly surface look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Walk it across the surface with alternating rod twitches; slow to a crawl near pad edges and let it rest fully before the next move. Keep it on top.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: The monthly surface window is still alive today, even with a little ripple on top. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Work it with medium strips that spit and gurgle; slow the cadence near structure and let the fly rest after each pop — target the edge of any surface shadow. Keep it on top.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: Heat stress moves fish to cooler, deeper water and narrows the feeding window. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Drag it along hard bottom and ledges so the flat head kicks and rocks; lift only slightly on the pull so the trailer stays near the substrate. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

scenarios.label: Midwest dirty backwater lake, prespawn opening month 3

expectation.seasonal_story: Midwest dirty backwater prespawn: football jig holds at position 0 via crawfish forage match plus dirty bonus; spinnerbait and bladed jig surface with dirty clarity boost but lack the forage alignment; suspending jerkbait can still linger as a lower-ranked control backup on nasty transition days.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Drag it along hard bottom and ledges so the flat head kicks and rocks; lift only slightly on the pull so the trailer stays near the substrate. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Drag it along hard bottom and ledges so the flat head kicks and rocks; lift only slightly on the pull so the trailer stays near the substrate. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: Cold metabolism shuts down activity. Slow and low is the only play. That keeps a crawfish-first look in the water for the month. It gives you a different cover weedless look without leaving today's window.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Flip or pitch into cover, let the craw fall straight on semi-slack line, then hop it once or twice before the next pitch. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. Lipless Crankbait stays in play when baitfish is relevant. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Yo-yo it through open water: rip up sharply, let it flutter and sink on a semi-slack line, then rip again; vary fall depth by watching the line angle. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: With the highest lane shut down, this keeps you just under the cleaner active zone. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Let it sink to depth then retrieve with slow hand-twists or short strips; the marabou tail breathes best with long pauses at the end of each pull. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: A sharp cooldown reinforces a lower daily lane. It stays low in the zone where this day still wants fish to hold. It gives you a different fly bottom look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Bump bottom with short strips and let the fly tick along rocks; keep tension so you feel every stop against structure. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: A sharp cooldown reinforces a lower daily lane. It stays low in the zone where this day still wants fish to hold. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Let it sink to the strike zone then retrieve with long, slow strips; the rabbit hair undulates seductively between pulls, so don't rush the retrieve. Slow down and lengthen the pause.

scenarios.label: Midwest dirty backwater lake, summer positioning month 7

expectation.seasonal_story: Midwest dirty backwater summer: walking topwater and prop bait both drop off in dirty water; popping topwater, buzzbait, hollow body frog, and swim jig carry the real dirty-water surface and cover bonuses.

top_1_lure.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_lure.how_to_fish: Cast past targets and swim it through, reeling just fast enough to feel the head wiggle; slow down near the edge of cover and let it drop. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Swim it at the speed where you feel a steady kick through the rod; use rod angle to change depth without changing retrieve speed. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_lures.why_chosen: The monthly surface window is still alive today, even with a little ripple on top. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_lures.how_to_fish: Twitch it just enough to make the legs kick; pause over every opening in the cover and count to three before moving — strikes on mats come after the pause. Keep it on top.

top_1_fly.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_1_fly.how_to_fish: Strip with alternating long and short pulls so the articulated body undulates differently each time; kill the retrieve if you see a fish following and let it sink. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: Moderate chop improves fishability and supports a stronger moving look. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Strip with alternating long and short pulls so the articulated body undulates differently each time; kill the retrieve if you see a fish following and let it sink. Keep it high in the zone.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: The monthly surface window is still alive today, even with a little ripple on top. It is one of the lead monthly looks for this exact seasonal window. It gives you a different fly surface look without leaving today's window.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Walk it across the surface with alternating rod twitches; slow to a crawl near pad edges and let it rest fully before the next move. Keep it on top.

top_3_flies.why_chosen: The monthly surface window is still alive today, even with a little ripple on top. The month is still baitfish-forward, and this stays inside that search lane. It is the cleaner change-up if the lead look does not convert.
top_3_flies.how_to_fish: Swim it on a constant slow retrieve just fast enough to leave a wake; target near-shore edges and structure where big fish expect food to cross. Keep it on top.
